description: Letter from Yiṣḥaq b. Nissim Farsī to Abū Zikrī Yaḥyā b. Mevorakh. Recto consists entirely of a panegyric and flattering rhymed prose in Hebrew. The addressee had previously sent a letter containing a poem either by Shelomo Ibn Gabirol or based on a model by Shelomo ibn Gabirol. Greetings are sent to the addressee’s father and mother, and he is encouraged to continue in his own poetic endeavors, said to be superior to those of the writer. On verso, the letter continues in Judaeo-Arabic. (Information from CUDL, Decter, and Mediterranean Society, V, pp. 197, 286–87, 562.) Recto was also edited by Ratzhaby, "שני שירים ספרדיים מאוצר הגניזה," Moznaim 6 (1992), 10–13.
description_he: מכתב בHebrew, Judaeo-Arabic. מוחזק בספריית קיימברידג'.
